entry.title: Online tool to read base64 encoded gzip strings to decompress EF Migration History EDMX

link.title: Online tool to read base64 encoded gzip strings to decompress EF Migration History EDMX

entry.summary: Motivation While working with EF migrations, I found it quite irritating to view the EDMX that Entity framework produces in the Migration History table. It is not really an issue when you’re already connected to that database context, as some C# code can be written to decompress it and return it as an XDocument. For example, private static XDocument Decompress(byte[] bytes) { using (var m...

entry.title: How to change the GDM login screen background

link.title: How to change the GDM login screen background

entry.summary: Environment OS: Ubuntu 19.10 x86_64 Host: XPS 15 9570 DE: GNOME Procedure Open the following file using your favorite editor with root access (e.g. vim, nano, gedit, code). sudo vim /usr/share/gnome-shell/theme/gnome-shell.css Search for something like this #lockDialogGroup { background: #2c001e url(resource:///org/gnome/shell/theme/noise-texture.png); background-repeat: repeat; } Ch...

entry.title: Gnome search shows no results, but hitting enter will actually launch the application

link.title: Gnome search shows no results, but hitting enter will actually launch the application

entry.summary: Environment OS: Ubuntu 19.10 x86_64 Host: XPS 15 9570 DE: GNOME Problem When you hit the windows key and start typing to search for something within gnome, there are no results. However, when you hit enter it will in fact launch the application that was the top search result. Solution This is probably caused by an update from 18.04 LTS. You were a ricing and installed a bunch of GDM themes. T...

entry.title: Making the Realforce 87UB work with USB 3.0 on Ubuntu

link.title: Making the Realforce 87UB work with USB 3.0 on Ubuntu

entry.summary: Environment OS: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS x86_64 Host: XPS 15 9570 Kernel: 5.4.0-33-generic DE: GNOME Problem behavior Your Realforce 87u or any other (older) Topre keyboard does not work under Ubunu / Linux, but does work under Windows, Grub, or the BIOS on that specific machine. $ dmesg | grep usb will probably give you something like this, where it will try to read the configuration a bunch of time...
